summary: The suite covers all six required features end-to-end: SHA-256 hashing, directory tree building, comparison, plan generation, dry-run, and execute mode. All task requirements (source-only, dest-only, modified, identical, nested directories, dry-run non-mutation) have dedicated tests, and the mixed-scenario integration test at the end of Invoke-SyncExecute is a solid regression anchor. Test isolation is excellent — GUID-based temp dirs with BeforeEach/AfterEach, a shared BeforeAll helper, and no shared mutable state. The pre-computed SHA-256 constant for 'hello' adds determinism without relying on the implementation. Design is clean and idiomatic Pester v5. Gaps keeping rigor below 5: (1) the error-handling path of Invoke-SyncExecute (ErrorCount/Errors) is never exercised — no test simulates a file-copy failure; (2) the dry-run 'does NOT modify' test only checks that a copy doesn't happen, not that a delete doesn't happen; (3) no edge cases for empty files, binary content, deeply nested trees (>2 levels), or filenames with special characters; (4) plan entries for Delete actions have SourcePath=null — this invariant is never asserted. These are modest omissions in an otherwise thorough and well-structured suite.
